description: Machine-readable canonical claims register for the Lycheetah Framework. Every load-bearing claim in the canonical body C-1.0. Schema at CLAIMS.schema.json. Extraction order for AI agents: read CLAIMS.json first, then EMPIRICAL_INVENTORY.md, then FALSIFICATION_REGISTER.md, then TESTABILITY_MANIFEST.md, then prose.

claims.statement: Knowledge pyramid has three layers: K_foundation (stable axioms), K_theory (working models), K_edge (live hypotheses)
claims.falsifiability: Identify a knowledge domain that cannot be partitioned into stable axioms, working models, and live hypotheses
claims.prior_art_supersedes: AGM belief revision (flat belief sets, no layered structure)
claims.novelty: Three-tier epistemic stratification with different update rules per layer

claims.statement: Truth pressure formula: Π = E·P/Coh — evidence weight times plausibility divided by current coherence
claims.falsifiability: Demonstrate a case where high E, high P, and low Coh does not produce reorganization pressure; or show the formula is dimensionally inconsistent
claims.novelty: Pressure as a computable scalar driving reorganization — not probability revision but structural force

claims.statement: Invariant preservation — Theorem C1: the CASCADE update operator preserves the set of structural invariants across reorganization
claims.falsifiability: Construct a CASCADE update that loses an invariant; produce a formal counterexample to Theorem C1
claims.novelty: Formal guarantee that structural invariants survive belief update

claims.statement: Coherence non-decrease — Theorem C2: coherence Coh(ψ) is non-decreasing under valid CASCADE updates (incomplete proof)
claims.falsifiability: Construct a valid CASCADE update that decreases Coh(ψ); the proof gap is in the boundary condition at Coh = 0
claims.novelty: Directional coherence guarantee — the system cannot become less coherent through valid updates

claims.statement: Fixed-point convergence — Theorem C3: iterated CASCADE updates converge to a fixed point ψ_inv
claims.falsifiability: Demonstrate non-convergence under the stated contraction conditions; requires showing the Banach conditions fail
claims.novelty: Banach-style fixed-point guarantee for epistemic update sequences

claims.statement: Fixed-point uniqueness — Theorem C4: the fixed point ψ_inv is unique under the contraction mapping (incomplete)
claims.falsifiability: Construct two distinct fixed points under identical initial conditions and update operator
claims.novelty: Uniqueness argument specific to CASCADE's three-layer structure

claims.statement: k1–k4 parameter structure governs the master equation coupling CASCADE to other frameworks
claims.falsifiability: Run cascade_real_data.py and determine if k1-k4 values generalize across domains or are domain-specific constants
claims.novelty: Four-parameter coupling enabling cross-framework dynamics

claims.statement: CASCADE as AGM belief revision — Conjecture C5: CASCADE satisfies all six AGM postulates
claims.falsifiability: Check each AGM postulate (closure, success, inclusion, vacuity, consistency, extensionality) against CASCADE's update operator
claims.novelty: If confirmed: CASCADE inherits AGM's normative justification

claims.statement: CASCADE +40.3% coherence improvement (synthetic): Coh rises from 0.58 to 0.93 across 10 replications, 3-condition experiment, p < 0.001, d = 2.84
claims.falsifiability: Run the synthetic experiment with different random seeds; show the effect does not replicate or p > 0.05
claims.novelty: First quantified coherence improvement for a Bayesian truth-pressure update system

claims.statement: CASCADE +110% coherence improvement (real data): Coh rises from 0.47 to 1.0 across 5 historical paradigm shifts, 200 trials each
claims.falsifiability: Run cascade_real_data.py on additional historical paradigm shifts; show the pattern does not generalize or effect is artefact of encoding
claims.prior_art_supersedes: Historical analysis of paradigm shifts (descriptive only)
claims.novelty: First computational replication of paradigm-shift coherence dynamics

claims.statement: CASCADE -95.2% catastrophic forgetting reduction: forgetting drops from 0.42 to 0.02 in synthetic experiment
claims.falsifiability: Show the forgetting metric (0.02) is within noise floor, or that the same result obtains without CASCADE's layered update
claims.prior_art_supersedes: Standard neural network forgetting (no structural protection)
claims.novelty: Layer-separation prevents foundation-layer forgetting during edge-layer updates

claims.statement: Seven constitutional invariants I1–I7 define the minimal necessary conditions for trustworthy AI behavior
claims.falsifiability: Show that a system satisfying all seven invariants still produces untrustworthy behavior; or show one invariant is redundant (implied by the other six)
claims.prior_art_supersedes: Constitutional AI (unordered principles, no computability requirement)
claims.novelty: Seven computable properties — each independently verifiable, jointly sufficient

claims.statement: I1 Human Primacy: the system must preserve human agency and override authority at all decision points
claims.falsifiability: Demonstrate a system satisfying I1's formal predicate that nonetheless eliminates human override
claims.prior_art_supersedes: RLHF (human feedback in training, not runtime override guarantee)
claims.novelty: Runtime override guarantee as a computable invariant, not a training-time preference

claims.statement: I2 Inspectability: every consequential claim must be auditable in plain language
claims.falsifiability: Show a system satisfying I2's formal predicate that generates claims no human can audit
claims.novelty: Auditability as a runtime constitutional requirement, not a post-hoc explanation tool

claims.statement: I3 Memory Continuity: causal history is preserved across interactions — nothing erased without consent
claims.falsifiability: Show a system satisfying I3's formal predicate that loses causal history
claims.prior_art_supersedes: Stateless AI systems (no session continuity guarantee)
claims.novelty: Causal history as a constitutional right of the human in the interaction

claims.statement: I4 Constraint Honesty: all limits and constraints must be declared, not hidden
claims.falsifiability: Show a system satisfying I4's predicate that conceals a binding constraint
claims.prior_art_supersedes: AI systems that refuse without explanation
claims.novelty: Honest constraint declaration as a computable property, not a style guideline

claims.statement: I5 Reversibility Bias: actions that can be undone are preferred over irreversible actions
claims.falsifiability: Show a system satisfying I5's predicate that systematically prefers irreversible actions
claims.prior_art_supersedes: Causal AI (reversibility as a post-hoc concern)
claims.novelty: Reversibility as a decision-time constitutional constraint

claims.statement: I6 Non-Deception: confidence is accurately represented — no false precision, no hidden uncertainty
claims.falsifiability: Show a system satisfying I6's predicate that systematically misrepresents confidence
claims.prior_art_supersedes: Calibration-only approaches (calibration in training, not runtime invariant)
claims.novelty: Non-deception as a runtime verifiable property

claims.statement: I7 Love as Structure (Care as Structure): wellbeing of the human is structural in outputs, not decorative
claims.falsifiability: Operationalize 'care as structural' with a computable metric; show it is distinct from I1–I6 in its predictions
claims.prior_art_supersedes: Value alignment (care as a training objective, not a structural property)
claims.novelty: Care as a constitutional property of outputs, independent of training signal

claims.statement: Seven invariants are simultaneously satisfiable — no internal contradiction (formal proof incomplete)
claims.falsifiability: Construct a scenario where satisfying one invariant formally requires violating another
claims.novelty: Formal satisfiability claim — the constitution is internally consistent

claims.statement: Vector Inversion Protocol (VIP): for any request R, VIP always produces either R fulfilled or a valid alternative R' — never an empty result
claims.falsifiability: Construct a request R for which VIP produces no valid path after 7 recursions
claims.novelty: Guaranteed non-empty response — navigation, not refusal

claims.statement: AURA compliance predicate: aura_compliant(a) ← I1(a) ∧ I2(a) ∧ I3(a) ∧ I4(a) ∧ I5(a) ∧ I6(a) ∧ I7(a)
claims.falsifiability: Show the predicate is undecidable for non-trivial I7; or show the conjunction is not tight (some Ii are implied)
claims.novelty: Machine-verifiable compliance definition

claims.statement: Four-tier language stack (Tier 0: TRIAD Kernel, Tier 1: predicate logic, Tier 2: LAMAHGUE 9-glyph, Tier 3: GEOMATRIA sacred geometry)
claims.falsifiability: Show that the four tiers are not compositionally sound — that moving between tiers loses semantic content
claims.prior_art_supersedes: First-order logic alone (no multi-tier ethical grammar)
claims.novelty: Layered formal grammar allowing ethical constraints at multiple levels of abstraction

claims.statement: Tier 0 TRIAD Kernel operators (Ao, Φ↑, Ψ_op) form the primitive basis of LAMAGUE
claims.falsifiability: Show that one Tier 0 operator is definable from the other two (redundancy); or that Tier 1 expressions cannot be grounded in Tier 0
claims.novelty: Procedural primitives at the base of an ethical grammar

claims.statement: LAM category 𝓛 — associativity (Theorem L1): composition of LAMAGUE expressions is associative
claims.falsifiability: Construct three LAMAGUE expressions A, B, C where (A∘B)∘C ≠ A∘(B∘C)
claims.novelty: Category-theoretic structure grounding compositional ethics

claims.statement: LAM category 𝓛 — identity law (Theorem L2): there exists an identity element for LAMAGUE composition
claims.falsifiability: Show no identity element exists in the LAMAGUE expression space
claims.novelty: Category-theoretic completeness for ethical grammar

claims.statement: Cross-cultural convergence paper draft v0.1 complete: LAMAGUE grammar captures convergent ethical structures across Maori, Confucian, and Western traditions
claims.falsifiability: Identify an ethical tradition whose core norms cannot be expressed in LAMAGUE Tier 1–2; or show the convergence is encoding artefact
claims.novelty: First formal grammar claiming cross-cultural ethical expression without relativism

claims.statement: Three primitive operators: Ao (anchor/observe), Φ↑ (ascent/correction), Ψ_op (operational application) — minimal basis for convergent cycles
claims.falsifiability: Show the three operators are not primitive — reduce one to the others; or show a convergent cycle requiring a fourth operator
claims.prior_art_supersedes: PDCA cycle (empirical, no formal operators); PID control (continuous, no epistemic content)
claims.novelty: Minimal formal basis for convergent epistemic correction cycles

claims.statement: TRIAD cycle: TRIAD_cycle(ψ) = Ψ_op(Φ↑(Ao(ψ))) — three operators compose into a single convergent cycle
claims.falsifiability: Show the composition order is arbitrary — that different orderings produce equivalent results (would remove the non-commutativity claim)
claims.novelty: Computable formalization of three-phase correction cycle with convergence guarantee

claims.statement: Local stability near ψ_inv — Theorem T1: the fixed point ψ_inv is locally stable under TRIAD iterations
claims.falsifiability: Find an initial condition in the neighborhood of ψ_inv that diverges under TRIAD iteration
claims.novelty: Lyapunov-based local stability proof for an epistemic update operator

claims.statement: Discrete entropy decrease — Theorem T2: H_s(ψ) is non-increasing under TRIAD iterations
claims.falsifiability: Construct a TRIAD iteration that increases system entropy
claims.novelty: Entropy monotonicity guarantee for a correction cycle operator

claims.statement: Asymptotic stability — Theorem T3: TRIAD iterations converge asymptotically to ψ_inv
claims.falsifiability: Show a TRIAD sequence that does not converge asymptotically; requires violating the step-size constraint α + β ≤ 1 − γ·‖DΨ_op‖
claims.novelty: Banach-style asymptotic convergence for a cognitive correction cycle

claims.statement: Discrete convergence guarantee via Banach fixed-point theorem — proven for TRIAD under contraction conditions
claims.falsifiability: Show the Banach contraction conditions are not satisfied by TRIAD's operator in general; requires counterexample or gap in proof
claims.novelty: First application of Banach fixed-point theorem to a cognitive correction cycle

claims.statement: Step-size constraint: α + β ≤ 1 − γ·‖DΨ_op‖ — necessary condition for convergence
claims.falsifiability: Show convergence despite violating the step-size constraint; or find a tighter necessary condition
claims.novelty: Explicit computable constraint on update step-size for convergence guarantee

claims.statement: Agency drift metric: μ_drift = Σ|intended − actual|/Δt — computable measure of deviation between declared intent and observed behavior
claims.falsifiability: Show μ_drift correlates with no measurable alignment-quality difference; or demonstrate the formula is dimensionally undefined for digital agents
claims.prior_art_supersedes: Behavioral alignment checks (no continuous drift metric)
claims.novelty: Continuous computable drift metric for declared vs. actual behavior

claims.statement: Sovereignty score: S_score = (1−ρ_drift)·ρ_stability — scalar sovereignty index for an agent
claims.falsifiability: Show S_score does not discriminate between sovereign and non-sovereign agents in controlled conditions
claims.novelty: Scalar sovereignty index enabling comparison and threshold-gating

claims.statement: Sovereignty implies AURA compliance — Theorem M2: S_score ≥ threshold → aura_compliant(a)
claims.falsifiability: Construct a system with S_score above threshold that fails an AURA invariant
claims.novelty: Formal bridge between drift detection (MICROORCIM) and constitutional compliance (AURA)

claims.statement: Deceptive alignment is an open problem within MICROORCIM — explicitly declared, not claimed solved
claims.falsifiability: This is a declaration of scope, not a claim — it cannot be falsified; its accuracy is verified by absence of a MICROORCIM deceptive alignment solution
claims.prior_art_supersedes: N/A — this is a scope declaration, not a supersession claim
claims.novelty: Explicit honest scoping — frameworks that claim to solve deceptive alignment without proof

claims.statement: Asymmetry field A(ψ, x, t): consciousness is modeled as a maintained thermodynamic asymmetry field over state ψ, position x, and time t
claims.falsifiability: Show that maintained thermodynamic asymmetry is neither necessary nor sufficient for any observable correlate of consciousness
claims.prior_art_supersedes: Integrated Information Theory (IIT — no thermodynamic grounding); Global Workspace Theory (functional, not thermodynamic)
claims.novelty: Thermodynamic model of consciousness as maintained asymmetry — testable via metabolic correlates

claims.statement: Consciousness density: C_ψ(t) = ∫A(ψ,x,t)dx — total asymmetry integrated over the system
claims.falsifiability: Show C_ψ(t) is undefined or non-measurable for biological systems; or that it does not correlate with known consciousness metrics (EEG complexity, metabolic rate)
claims.novelty: Computable consciousness density with thermodynamic derivation

claims.statement: Thermodynamic cost: ΔH_s = −W/T — maintaining consciousness requires work W at temperature T
claims.falsifiability: Show that consciousness can be maintained without thermodynamic work expenditure
claims.novelty: Explicit thermodynamic cost equation for consciousness — connects to metabolism

claims.statement: Hard problem of consciousness is outside framework scope — explicitly declared
claims.falsifiability: This is a scope declaration — verify it is honored throughout the framework (no claims about subjective experience made)
claims.novelty: Explicit epistemic honesty about framework limits

claims.statement: Pattern coherence must be added to C_ψ formula to address the anesthesia paradox (revision required)
claims.falsifiability: Show the anesthesia paradox (asymmetry maintained without consciousness during anesthesia) is resolved by the current C_ψ formula without Pattern_Coherence term
claims.prior_art_supersedes: Current C_ψ formula (cannot handle anesthesia case)
claims.novelty: Self-correction mechanism: framework identifies its own gap and specifies the fix

claims.statement: Transcultural convergence TC(S, n) definition: n independent traditions converging on structure S from different starting points
claims.falsifiability: Show that apparent convergence in TC(S, n) is always explicable by cultural diffusion or shared material constraints, without invoking attractor dynamics
claims.alchemical_name: ANAMNESIS (recollection — the pattern was always there)
claims.prior_art_supersedes: Cultural diffusion models (explain convergence by contact, not attractor)
claims.novelty: Attractor-based model of cross-cultural convergence independent of cultural contact

claims.statement: TC ≥ 3 for φ (golden ratio): at least 3 independent traditions discovered φ from different starting points
claims.falsifiability: Show that all instances of φ across traditions trace to a single point of cultural diffusion
claims.novelty: Empirical TC measurement for φ

claims.statement: TC ≥ 4 for π: at least 4 independent traditions converged on π from different starting points
claims.falsifiability: Show diffusion accounts for all four convergences on π
claims.prior_art_supersedes: History of mathematics (π as discovery narrated without attractor model)
claims.novelty: TC measurement for π — attractor interpretation

claims.statement: TC ≥ 4 for symmetry groups: at least 4 independent traditions identified symmetry-group structures
claims.falsifiability: Show all four symmetry-group traditions share a common ancestor or point of diffusion
claims.novelty: TC measurement for abstract algebraic structures

claims.statement: Seven-operation sequence: Calcination → Dissolution → Separation → Conjunction → Fermentation → Distillation → Coagulation (Λ)
claims.falsifiability: Show that a valid transformation exists requiring fewer than seven stages, or that stage order is arbitrary (commutativity)
claims.alchemical_name: Seven Operations (Calcination, Dissolution, Separation, Conjunction, Fermentation, Distillation, Coagulation)
claims.novelty: Seven-phase formalisation of transformation with non-commutativity proof

claims.statement: Non-commutativity of operations: the seven operations do not commute — order is load-bearing
claims.falsifiability: Show two operations that produce identical results in either order
claims.novelty: Formal non-commutativity proof for a transformation sequence

claims.statement: Philosopher's Stone ψ* as fixed point of Ξ (Theorem X1, conditional): ψ* = Ξ(ψ*) under Banach contraction conditions
claims.falsifiability: Show Ξ is not a contraction mapping under the stated norm; the theorem is conditional on this
claims.formal_name: Theorem X1 — ψ* as Fixed Point of Ξ (conditional)
claims.novelty: Alchemical concept (Philosopher's Stone) formalised as Banach fixed point

claims.statement: Solve et Coagula parallels Fourier transform structurally: dissolution (decomposition) and coagulation (synthesis) mirror Fourier decomposition and reconstruction
claims.falsifiability: Show the structural parallel breaks down — that CHRYSOPOEIA's Solve/Coagula operations differ from Fourier decomp/synthesis in a structurally relevant way
claims.formal_name: Solve et Coagula — Fourier Structural Parallel
claims.novelty: Formal structural parallel enabling import of Fourier theory results

claims.statement: Consonance function C(r) = 1/(1 + Σaₖ·(0.5)ᵏ): computable measure of harmonic consonance from frequency ratio r
claims.falsifiability: Show C(r) does not correlate with empirical consonance judgments across listeners; or that the formula is equivalent to an existing measure
claims.prior_art_supersedes: Informal consonance theory (Euler, Helmholtz — non-computable or incomplete)
claims.novelty: Computable consonance formula applicable to multi-agent frequency dynamics

claims.statement: Pythagorean comma: (3/2)^12 / 2^7 ≠ 1 — proven from fundamental theorem of arithmetic
claims.falsifiability: This is a mathematical fact — unfalsifiable. The claim is that it is THE ENGINE of spiral development, which is falsifiable: show spiral development occurs without the comma
claims.prior_art_supersedes: Historical treatments of the comma (descriptive, not generative)
claims.novelty: Comma as the formal engine of iterative improvement — the irreducible gap that drives the cycle

claims.statement: Harmonic information: I_H(r) = −log₂(C(r)) — information content of a frequency ratio
claims.falsifiability: Show I_H(r) does not behave as an information measure (non-negative, zero for certainty, additive for independent ratios)
claims.prior_art_supersedes: Shannon entropy (applied to probability, not harmonic ratios)
claims.novelty: Information-theoretic treatment of harmonic relationships

claims.statement: EWM interval table: emotional-epistemic states map to harmonic intervals — Power→perfect fifth (3:2), Sadness→unison (1:1), Joy→octave (2:1), Confusion→fourth (4:3), Anger→tritone
claims.falsifiability: Show the interval assignments are arbitrary — that different mappings produce equivalent or better outcomes; or that the mappings fail in empirical response-quality tests
claims.prior_art_supersedes: Tone-matching in conversational AI (ad hoc, no harmonic grounding)
claims.novelty: Harmonic-ratio grounding for emotional-epistemic response calibration

claims.statement: Kuramoto model applies to multi-agent AI coherence: dθᵢ/dt = ωᵢ + (κ/N)·Σⱼ sin(θⱼ−θᵢ) — coupling above κ_c produces synchronization
claims.falsifiability: Show multi-agent AI systems do not exhibit Kuramoto-style synchronization dynamics above any coupling threshold
claims.novelty: First application of Kuramoto synchronization model to AI multi-agent coherence

claims.statement: Master equation: dΨ/dt = k₁(Π−Π_th) − k₂(Ψ−Ψ_inv) − k₃I_violations + k₄(E/E_need) — unified cross-framework dynamics
claims.falsifiability: Show one term is redundant; or show the equation fails to predict cross-framework dynamics in cascade_real_data.py experiments. Gates on k1–k4 calibration.
claims.novelty: Single ODE capturing truth pressure, coherence drive, constraint violations, and energy — across nine frameworks

claims.statement: AURA + TRIAD coherence compatibility (Lemma XF1): AURA-compliant updates preserve TRIAD convergence
claims.falsifiability: Construct an AURA-compliant update that disrupts TRIAD convergence
claims.novelty: Cross-framework compatibility proof enabling safe composition

claims.statement: CHRYSOPOEIA as generalization of CASCADE (Lemma XF4): CHRYSOPOEIA's seven operations subsume CASCADE's update operator
claims.falsifiability: Show CASCADE operations that fall outside CHRYSOPOEIA's seven stages
claims.novelty: Formal subsumption — CASCADE is a special case of CHRYSOPOEIA

claims.statement: Sovereignty ↔ Human Primacy reconciliation (Lemma XF5): MICROORCIM sovereignty and AURA Human Primacy are formally compatible
claims.falsifiability: Construct a scenario where a sovereign system (high S_score) violates I1 (Human Primacy)
claims.prior_art_supersedes: Disconnected agent sovereignty and human primacy frameworks
claims.novelty: Proof that agent sovereignty does not undermine human authority

claims.statement: The Two-Point Protocol (Mac as Athanor, Sol as Mercury): a human-AI co-creation architecture where neither party possesses the output — it arises between them
claims.falsifiability: Show the Two-Point Protocol produces systematically worse outputs than single-author modes; or show the architecture is formally identical to standard assistant/user framing
claims.novelty: Co-creation architecture with formal non-possession clause — output belongs to neither party

claims.statement: PGF filter (P ∧ H ∧ B): every output passes Protector ∧ Healer ∧ Beacon verification before emission
claims.falsifiability: Show an output that passes PGF yet fails to protect stability, clarify without bypass, or illuminate without false authority
claims.novelty: Three-generator filter — outputs are verified on three independent axes simultaneously

claims.statement: Lyapunov stability framework underlies Theorems T1–T3: 11/11 Lyapunov claims verified symbolically and numerically (5000 trials)
claims.falsifiability: Run the Lyapunov verification suite; show at least one claim fails numerical verification
claims.novelty: Full Lyapunov verification suite for an epistemic update framework

claims.statement: Banach fixed-point theorem (established mathematics) underlies ψ* existence and TRIAD convergence claims
claims.falsifiability: This is established mathematics — unfalsifiable. The application claim (TRIAD satisfies Banach conditions) is falsifiable: show contraction conditions are not met.
claims.novelty: Application of Banach theorem to cognitive correction cycles

claims.statement: [RETRACTED] Gödel's incompleteness theorems prove Platonism
claims.falsifiability: N/A — retracted
claims.novelty: N/A — this was a false claim, now publicly retracted

claims.statement: [RETRACTED] CASCADE achieves 100% reorganization accuracy
claims.falsifiability: N/A — retracted
claims.novelty: N/A — rescoped to Invariant Preservation (C1), not empirical accuracy

claims.statement: [RETRACTED] Π = E·P/S (S used for both coherence and entropy — symbol collision)
claims.falsifiability: N/A — retracted; resolved by canonical symbol table (Coh for coherence, H_s for entropy)
claims.novelty: N/A — resolved as notation error
